message:
feat(core): wrap ext4_add_dir_entry in MVCC transaction for atomic directory block allocation

The entire ext4_add_dir_entry method — which handles both "add entry to
existing block" and "allocate new block + insert extent + write entry" paths —
is now wrapped in an MVCC transaction. Previously, the new-block allocation
path wrote directly to the base block device, meaning a crash between the
block write and the extent tree insertion could leave an allocated-but-
unreferenced directory block (space leak) or a referenced-but-uninitialized
block (corruption).

Now begins a transaction before the entry scan, routes all write_block and
write_inode calls through TransactionBlockAdapter, and commits only after
all mutations (dir entry, extent tree, parent inode) succeed. If any step
fails, the transaction is never committed, leaving the base device untouched.

Also moves extent tree insertion and parent inode update for the new-block
path inside the transaction closure so they share the same adapter and
read-your-own-writes consistency.

ffs-dir: Adds tracing::debug for rec_len/name_len/file_type parsing to aid
directory corruption diagnosis.

ffs-harness: Removes duplicate tracing_subscriber init that was causing a
test panic (subscriber already set).

ffs-cli: Adds temporary debug println for log buffer inspection in
parse_first_json_line test helper (will be removed in follow-up).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
